name: FP200 Gold (WS52W)
notes: Industry default for fire-alarm cabling in NSW. Solid copper conductors, ceramifiable insulation. Support spacing per manufacturer; usually ≤ 0.45 m for clipped runs.

name: Mineral-Insulated Metal-Sheathed (MIMS / MICC)
notes: Older copper-jacketed alternative to FP200 — heavier, harder to terminate. Used where mechanical durability is critical (plant rooms).

name: Fire-rated Steel Wire Armoured (FR-SWA)
notes: Mechanical armour for buried or external runs. NOT a substitute for WS52W on the SLC itself. Verify the specific construction's AS 3013 compliance.

name: FP200 Flex (multi-strand)
notes: Use for short flexible terminations only. Do not run as a loop — solid-core for fixed runs.

name: Standard TPS (NOT fire-rated)
use_cases: NEVER for fire detection / EWIS / brigade circuits
notes: DO NOT USE for any fire-system circuit. Listed here so techs can identify it on retrofit work — find it in old buildings, replace with WS52W.

name: Cat6 / Cat6A data
notes: Non-fire-rated. Use for monitoring console links, not for SLC or output circuits. LSZH variant for plenum runs.
